name: tuw_multi_robot_msgs
description: The tuw_multi_robot_msgs package contains messages for sending graph, route and sync data over topics.

Graph.description: This message is used to transport a graph between two nodes.
Graph.raw:
#################################################################
## This message is used to transport a graph between two nodes.
## Each vertex has a set of points and a width describing the
## shape of it and additionally successors and predecessors
## to describe the overall structure of the graph
##
## Remark for the TUW_Multi_Robot_Router
## !! all vertices have to be sorted by id
## !! every id must be present...
#################################################################

std_msgs/Header header      # Header for Timestamp and Id
geometry_msgs/Pose origin   # The origin of the map (respectively: offset (the map is moved))
Vertex[] vertices           # The vertices in the graph (in pixels)

fields.name: header
fields.comment: Header for Timestamp and Id

fields.name: origin
fields.comment: The origin of the map (respectively: offset (the map is moved))

fields.name: vertices
fields.comment: The vertices in the graph (in pixels)

Order.description: Good to deliver from position A to B (via intermediate
Order.raw:
#################################################################
## Good to deliver from position A to B (via intermediate
## positions)
#################################################################

std_msgs/Header        header
int32                  order_id
string                 order_name
Station[]              stations

Pickup.description: notifies robot to pick up good

RobotGoals.description: used to describe a set of destinations for a robot
RobotGoals.raw:
#################################################################
## used to describe a set of destinations for a robot
## robot_name is used as id and often used as namespace for topics
#################################################################

string robot_name                   # the name of the robot
geometry_msgs/Pose[] destinations   # Describes a set of destinations, where the last one is the final goal of the trip
                                    # if there are more than one destinations the first one is the start pose
                                    # else the current pose of the robot is used as start

fields.name: robot_name
fields.comment: the name of the robot

fields.name: destinations
fields.comment: Describes a set of destinations, where the last one is the final goal of the trip

RobotGoalsArray.description: Used to send goal requests for multiple robots to the multi robot planner
RobotGoalsArray.raw:
#################################################################
## Used to send goal requests for multiple robots to the multi robot planner
#################################################################

std_msgs/Header header  # the time of the generation
RobotGoals[] robots     # contains a goals/checkpoints for each robot

fields.name: header
fields.comment: the time of the generation

fields.name: robots
fields.comment: contains a goals/checkpoints for each robot

RobotInfo.description: Presents dynamic parameters of a robot
RobotInfo.raw:
#################################################################
## Presents dynamic parameters of a robot
#################################################################

std_msgs/Header header                   # the creation time
string robot_name                        # the name of the robot (used in preconditions and topics)
geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance pose    # the robots current pose within the frame related to the msgs header
int32 shape                              # the shape of the robots (see enums)
float32[] shape_variables                # shape variables to define width height, ...
RoutePrecondition sync                   # the current position in the last received plan (-1 means none)
int32   mode                             # the mode of operation
int32   status                           # the status of the robot
int32   good_id                          # the good id attached to the robot
int32   order_id                         # the order id scheduled to this robot (-1: none)
int32   order_status                     # the status of the assigned order

# mode
int32 MODE_NA = 0                   # undefined mode
int32 MODE_IDLE = 1                 # robot is idle
int32 MODE_SEGMENT_FOLLOWING = 2    # robot is in mode segment following
int32 MODE_PICKUP = 3               # robot is picking up goods

# status
int32 STATUS_DRIVING = 0            # robot is driving
int32 STATUS_STOPPED = 1            # robot has stopped
int32 STATUS_DONE = 2               # robot has finished its last job
int32 STATUS_BROKEN = 3             # robot is broken and not ready for any task

# good_id
int32 GOOD_EMPTY = -1               # no goods attached
int32 GOOD_NA = -2                  # undefined good

# shape
int32 SHAPE_CIRCLE = 0                 # robot is in shape of a circle    ShapeVars

# order_status
int32 ORDER_NONE = 0                # no order assigned
int32 ORDER_APPROACH = 1            # the robot approaches the first station of the order
int32 ORDER_PICKUP = 2              # the robot picks up a good at the station
int32 ORDER_TRANSPORT = 3           # the robot currently transports a good from one station to another
int32 ORDER_DROP = 4                # the robot drops a good at the last station of its order, finishing the order

fields.name: header
fields.comment: the creation time

fields.name: robot_name
fields.comment: the name of the robot (used in preconditions and topics)

fields.name: pose
fields.comment: the robots current pose within the frame related to the msgs header

fields.name: shape
fields.comment: the shape of the robots (see enums)

fields.name: shape_variables
fields.comment: shape variables to define width height, ...

fields.name: sync
fields.comment: the current position in the last received plan (-1 means none)

fields.name: mode
fields.comment: the mode of operation

fields.name: status
fields.comment: the status of the robot

fields.name: good_id
fields.comment: the good id attached to the robot

fields.name: order_id
fields.comment: the order id scheduled to this robot (-1: none)

fields.name: order_status
fields.comment: the status of the assigned order

fields.name: MODE_NA
fields.comment: undefined mode

fields.name: MODE_IDLE
fields.comment: robot is idle

fields.name: MODE_SEGMENT_FOLLOWING
fields.comment: robot is in mode segment following

fields.name: MODE_PICKUP
fields.comment: robot is picking up goods

fields.name: STATUS_DRIVING
fields.comment: robot is driving

fields.name: STATUS_STOPPED
fields.comment: robot has stopped

fields.name: STATUS_DONE
fields.comment: robot has finished its last job

fields.name: STATUS_BROKEN
fields.comment: robot is broken and not ready for any task

fields.name: GOOD_EMPTY
fields.comment: no goods attached

fields.name: GOOD_NA
fields.comment: undefined good

fields.name: SHAPE_CIRCLE
fields.comment: robot is in shape of a circle    ShapeVars

fields.name: ORDER_NONE
fields.comment: no order assigned

fields.name: ORDER_APPROACH
fields.comment: the robot approaches the first station of the order

fields.name: ORDER_PICKUP
fields.comment: the robot picks up a good at the station

fields.name: ORDER_TRANSPORT
fields.comment: the robot currently transports a good from one station to another

fields.name: ORDER_DROP
fields.comment: the robot drops a good at the last station of its order, finishing the order

Route.description: used to guide a single vehicle along segments
Route.raw:
#################################################################
## used to guide a single vehicle along segments
#################################################################
std_msgs/Header header   # time of route generation
RouteSegment[] segments  # route segments a robot has to follow

fields.name: header
fields.comment: time of route generation

fields.name: segments
fields.comment: route segments a robot has to follow

RoutePrecondition.description: Route Preconditions are used to sync robots on a route
RoutePrecondition.raw:
#################################################################
## Route Preconditions are used to sync robots on a route
## e.g.: Each robot publishes its current step of its route
## with such a message
## The specific segments of a route are marked with such
## preconditions to block a robot from entering a segment
## until the sync message of the other robot has the right
## route_segment_nr
#################################################################

string robot_id                  # the robot name for the precondition
int32 current_route_segment      # the segment nr of the route executed by the given robot

fields.name: robot_id
fields.comment: the robot name for the precondition

fields.name: current_route_segment
fields.comment: the segment nr of the route executed by the given robot

RouteProgress.raw:
std_msgs/Header header           
int32[] passed   # A list of already passed route segment id's.
int32 current    # The route segment id where the robot is currently located.
int32[] todo     # A list of route segment id's which will be visited by the robot. If this list is empty, the robot has finished its route.
float32 progress        # The route progress in %.

fields.name: passed
fields.comment: A list of already passed route segment id's.

fields.name: current
fields.comment: The route segment id where the robot is currently located.

fields.name: todo
fields.comment: A list of route segment id's which will be visited by the robot. If this list is empty, the robot has finished its route.

fields.name: progress
fields.comment: The route progress in %.

RouteSegment.description: Describes a segment on a route with: start, end, width
RouteSegment.raw:
#################################################################
## Describes a segment on a route with: start, end, width
## and preconditions for synchronisation to other robots
#################################################################

int32 segment_id                        # the unique identifier of a segment
RoutePrecondition[] preconditions       # the preconditions, which have to be met before entering a segment
geometry_msgs/Pose start                # startpoint of the segment
geometry_msgs/Pose end                  # endpoint of the segment
float32 width                           # width of the segment

fields.name: segment_id
fields.comment: the unique identifier of a segment

fields.name: preconditions
fields.comment: the preconditions, which have to be met before entering a segment

fields.name: start
fields.comment: startpoint of the segment

fields.name: end
fields.comment: endpoint of the segment

fields.name: width
fields.comment: width of the segment

RouterStatus.description: A message to publish the status of the last route
RouterStatus.raw:
#################################################################
## A message to publish the status of the last route
## generation
#################################################################

int32 id # the unique id of the new plan
bool success # true if a routing table was found to the given scenario
string[] missing_robots # if the plan fails because of absent robots, these robots are listed here
int32 duration # the time until a routing table was found
int32 overall_path_length # the overall path length in the routing table (calculated using weights)
int32 longest_path_length # the longest path length in a routing table (calculated using weights)
int32 priority_scheduling_attemps # shows how often robot priorities are exchanged
int32 speed_scheduling_attemps # shows how many robots speed was reduced during planning

fields.name: id
fields.comment: the unique id of the new plan

fields.name: success
fields.comment: true if a routing table was found to the given scenario

fields.name: missing_robots
fields.comment: if the plan fails because of absent robots, these robots are listed here

fields.name: duration
fields.comment: the time until a routing table was found

fields.name: overall_path_length
fields.comment: the overall path length in the routing table (calculated using weights)

fields.name: longest_path_length
fields.comment: the longest path length in a routing table (calculated using weights)

fields.name: priority_scheduling_attemps
fields.comment: shows how often robot priorities are exchanged

fields.name: speed_scheduling_attemps
fields.comment: shows how many robots speed was reduced during planning

Station.description: Station for Robots to Drive to
Station.raw:
#################################################################
## Station for Robots to Drive to
#################################################################

std_msgs/Header        header       # header
int32                  id           # station ID
string                 name         # station name
geometry_msgs/Pose     pose         # station position

fields.name: header
fields.comment: header

fields.name: id
fields.comment: station ID

fields.name: name
fields.comment: station name

fields.name: pose
fields.comment: station position

StationArray.description: Array of Stations for Robots to Drive to

fields.name: header
fields.comment: header

fields.name: stations
fields.comment: array of stations

Vertex.description: A single vertex in a graph
Vertex.raw:
#################################################################
## A single vertex in a graph
## Each vertex of the same graph must have a unique id.
## Successors and Predecessors must have a common start or end
## point
#################################################################
uint32 id                   # Vertex id
bool valid                  # true if it can be used for planning
geometry_msgs/Point[] path  # points describing a path from the vertex start to the vertex endpoint
                            #    the first point in the array reprecents the start and the last the endpoint
                            #    this points can also be used by the vehciles local path following algorithm 
uint32 weight               # the weight of the vertex (e.g. length of the segment)
float32 width               # fee space next to the vertex
uint32[] successors         # edges to successors
uint32[] predecessors       # edges to predecessor

fields.name: id
fields.comment: Vertex id

fields.name: valid
fields.comment: true if it can be used for planning

fields.name: path
fields.comment: points describing a path from the vertex start to the vertex endpoint

fields.name: weight
fields.comment: the weight of the vertex (e.g. length of the segment)

fields.name: width
fields.comment: fee space next to the vertex

fields.name: successors
fields.comment: edges to successors

fields.name: predecessors
fields.comment: edges to predecessor

StationManagerControlProtocol.description: Protocol to Send and Receive Station Management Commands
StationManagerControlProtocol.raw:
#################################################################
## Protocol to Send and Receive Station Management Commands
#################################################################

string      request         # request
string      addition        # addition, holds information to the request

# request constants
string LOAD = load          # command to load a file (addition is the file path)
string SAVE = save          # command to save a file (addition is the file path)
string UPDATE = update      # command to define update policy

string ONCE = once          # addition to publish data once on request
string CHANGE = change      # addition to publish data on ever change

---

string      response        # response string

# response constants
string EXECUTED = executed  # response if the request could be executed successful
string FAILED = failed      # response if the request could not be executed successful
string ERROR = error        # response if the request is unknown

request_fields.name: addition
request_fields.comment: addition, holds information to the request

request_fields.name: LOAD
request_fields.comment: command to load a file (addition is the file path)

request_fields.name: SAVE
request_fields.comment: command to save a file (addition is the file path)

request_fields.name: ONCE
request_fields.comment: addition to publish data once on request

request_fields.name: CHANGE
request_fields.comment: addition to publish data on ever change

response_fields.name: EXECUTED
response_fields.comment: response if the request could be executed successful

response_fields.name: FAILED
response_fields.comment: response if the request could not be executed successful

response_fields.name: ERROR
response_fields.comment: response if the request is unknown

StationManagerStationProtocol.description: Protocol to Send and Receive Control Commands
StationManagerStationProtocol.raw:
#################################################################
## Protocol to Send and Receive Control Commands
#################################################################

string                          request         # request string
tuw_multi_robot_msgs/Station    station         # station message

# request constants
string APPEND = append                          # request to append sent station
string REMOVE = remove                          # request to remove sent station

---

string                          response        # response string

# response constants
string EXECUTED = executed                      # response if the request could be executed successful
string FAILED = failed                          # response if the request could not be executed successful
string ERROR = error                            # response if the request is unknown

response_fields.name: EXECUTED
response_fields.comment: response if the request could be executed successful

response_fields.name: FAILED
response_fields.comment: response if the request could not be executed successful

response_fields.name: ERROR
response_fields.comment: response if the request is unknown

FollowRoute.raw:
#goal definition
string behaviour_tree
tuw_multi_robot_msgs/Route route
---
#result definition
std_msgs/Empty result
---
#feedback definition
tuw_multi_robot_msgs/RouteProgress route_progress
